hypothesis: BTC and ETH exhibit persistent multi-week trends driven by retail sentiment, on-chain adoption cycles, and macro risk-on/risk-off flows. A 20/60-day moving average crossover captures these trends after initiation, filtering out short-term noise. Crypto trends are stronger and longer than equity trends due to the combination of high volatility and retail momentum-chasing behavior.
universe: BTC (Bitcoin) and ETH (Ethereum) spot or perpetual futures — the only crypto assets with sufficient liquidity, history, and exchange support for systematic trading. Altcoins explicitly excluded due to insufficient history and liquidity.
entry_logic: Go long BTC/ETH when the 20-day SMA crosses above the 60-day SMA (golden cross). Go flat (exit long) when 20-day crosses below 60-day. No short positions due to borrow cost and volatility risk in small accounts. Enter at next hourly close after signal confirmation.
exit_logic: Exit long on the death cross (20-day SMA crosses below 60-day SMA). Trailing stop: once in profit, set a trailing stop at 10% below the recent 30-day high. This allows riding strong trends while protecting gains.
position_sizing: Equal allocation between BTC and ETH (50/50 when both in uptrend). Each position maximum 15% of total portfolio. When only one signal is active, allocate up to 15% to that asset. Maximum 30% combined crypto allocation (per mission capital allocation targets). Maximum 2x leverage — strongly prefer no leverage in small account.
risk_management: Hard stop: exit immediately if position loses 8% from entry (crypto volatility is 3-5x equities). Position-level trailing stop at 10% below recent 30-day high once position is profitable. Reduce to 50% position size if BTC 30-day realized vol exceeds 80% annualized (extreme vol regime). Keep only trading capital on exchange — move excess to cold storage. No leverage above 2x.
known_failure_modes: Crypto winters: prolonged bear markets (2018, H2 2022) generate repeated whipsaw signals and cumulative losses as the MA system lags the down-trend.
Choppy regimes: sideways crypto markets with high volatility generate numerous false crossovers and transaction costs that erode capital.
Exchange risk: exchange failures (FTX 2022) can cause complete capital loss — only exchange-side trading capital, rest in cold storage.
24/7 markets: signals can trigger at illiquid hours (weekends, late night) with poor fills — use limit orders or only execute during high-liquidity windows.
Lag in trend capture: MA crossover is inherently a lagging indicator — a significant portion of the trend is missed before entry and a portion is given back on exit.

historical_performance.notes: Must include 2018 crypto winter in backtest (per Gate 1 criteria) — the strategy survived but had meaningful drawdown. 2020-2021 bull market produced excellent performance. 2022 bear caused the most significant drawdown. The trailing stop significantly improves risk/reward vs. pure crossover exit. ETH tends to have stronger trends but higher volatility than BTC.

references: quantopian/research_public - Lecture: Introduction to Trading Algorithms (MA Crossover basics)
Andreas Antonopoulos - Mastering Bitcoin (for market structure understanding)
Nansen Research - Crypto Market Structure Reports
